codeReview.summary: Coverage increased from 36.5% to 66.4% (+30pp). Tests cover critical paths in internal/client package. However, one test has a critical flaw where it doesn't actually verify what it claims to verify.

issues.description: TestDoRequestWithToken_Headers is supposed to verify request headers (Authorization, User-Agent, Accept) are set correctly, but headerCheckTransport.RoundTrip() does not validate the expectedHeaders field against actual request headers. It just returns a success response unconditionally. The test name and comments claim it verifies headers are set correctly, but no actual verification occurs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file internal/client/coverage_test.go follows Go testing conventions and project structure. No convention gaps found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: File is properly placed in internal/client/ directory, uses standard Go testing package, follows naming conventions

summary: The test coverage improvement is substantial (36.5% -> 66.4%, +30pp). Most tests are meaningful and verify actual behavior. However, TestDoRequestWithToken_Headers has a critical flaw: the headerCheckTransport.RoundTrip method accepts expectedHeaders but never actually validates them against the incoming request. The test passes but doesn't verify the headers it claims to verify.
